title: carmichaelia crassicaulis
intro: Carmichaelia crassicaulis, also known as the 'thick-stemmed broom', is a species of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae, native to New Zealand. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, due to its health benefits. Carmichaelia crassicaulis can be prepared as a herbal remedy through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, and it has also been used in poultices and salves.
